LAW AND MOTION 301, NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANT MARIA SAPPARRART'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT -- RE: 5TH C/A - OVERRULED; PLAINTIFF DOES NOT NEED TO VACATE PREMISES TO STATE A C/A FOR BREACH OF THE COVENANT OF QUIET ENJOYMENT. RE: 8TH C/A - OVERRULED; INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS IS SUFFICIENTLY PLEADED. RE: 9TH C/A - OVERRULED; NEGLIGENT INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTESS IS SUFFICIENTLY PLEADED. DEFENDANT HAS 10 DAYS LEAVE TO ANSWER. REPORTER: ROBERT BALIAN, CSR #5220

MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANT MARIA SAPPARRART'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT -- Re: 5th c/a - Overruled; plaintiff does not need to vacate premises to state a c/a for breach of the covenant of quiet enjoyment. Re: 8th c/a - Overruled; intentional infliction of emotional distress is sufficiently pleaded. Re: 9th c/a - Overruled; negligent infliction of emotional distess is sufficiently pleaded. Defendant has 10 days leave to answer. (PHA/301/EH) Reporter: Robert Balian, CSR #5220
